title: pseudotsuga menziesii subsp. oaxacana
intro: Pseudotsuga menziesii subsp. oaxacana, also known as the Oaxacan Douglas Fir, is a subspecies of the Douglas Fir tree native to the state of Oaxaca in Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Pseudotsuga menziesii subsp. oaxacana include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, and its essential oils can be extracted for further use in aromatherapy and traditional medicine.
